settings.name: The Living Room
settings.copy: Low sofas, a table that takes mugs without coasters, light you can read by.

settings.name: The Study
settings.copy: A desk that holds still while you think, and shelving that earns its wall.

settings.name: The Bedroom
settings.copy: A bed frame that doesn't creak and linen that gets better after every wash.

settings.name: Walnut
settings.sourcing: American black walnut, air-dried for two years before it meets a blade.

settings.name: Bouclé
settings.sourcing: A looped wool blend from a Belgian mill. It wears in, not out.

settings.name: Powder-coated steel
settings.sourcing: Bent, welded, and coated an hour from the workshop. No plastic caps to lose.

settings.question: What does white-glove delivery include?
settings.answer: We bring the piece inside, up the stairs, into the room, unwrap it, and take every scrap of packaging with us. You point; we place.

settings.question: Do I have to assemble anything?
settings.answer: Almost never. Most pieces arrive whole; the few that travel in parts, the delivery team finishes on the spot — bolts, levels, and all.

settings.question: How do returns work for large items?
settings.answer: You have 30 days. We collect the piece from the room we delivered it to — no re-boxing required. Made-to-order fabrics are the one exception, flagged clearly before you buy.
